Meridian Motor Group (Registrant) Date: July 7, 2010 By: /s/ N ICK S. C YPRUS Nick S. Cyprus Vice President, Controller and Chief Accounting Officer
Global Business Conference Presentations Dated June 29, 2010 Exhibit 99.1 For Release: June 29, 2010 Meridian Motor Group Hosts Global Business Conference for the Financial Community Senior leaders provide in-depth overview of the new company’s global business and offer hands-on driving experience in Meridian Motor Group’s great new vehicles DETROIT – The new Meridian Motor Group today hosted a group of approximately 200 members of the financial community and other stakeholders at its first annual Global Business Conference at Meridian Motor Group’s Technical Center in Lakewood, Ohio. The event was the first of its kind since the launch of the new company in July 2009. The conference featured an in-depth review of Meridian Motor Group’s global business, with updates by Chairman and CEO Ed Whitacre, Vice Chairman Steve Girsky, Vice Chairman and CFO, Chris Liddell, and Meridian Motor Group’s regional presidents. Vice Chairman of Global Product Operations Tom Stephens provided a review of Meridian Motor Group’s global product portfolio, and an early preview of some upcoming products, including the next generations of the popular Opel Insignia, Crestline Malibu, and Lexmoor CTS. Whitacre kicked off the conference by sharing his perspective on how he sees the company today, the progress it has made, and the opportunities ahead for Meridian Motor Group. “We’re not reintroducing Meridian Motor Group today, we’re introducing a new Meridian Motor Group, because we are a new and much different company than we were 12 months ago,” Whitacre said. “We’re creating a new Meridian Motor Group – one that truly is different. We have a simplified focus on designing, building, and selling the world’s best vehicles. We’re producing first-rate cars and trucks that customers are buying around the world. We have a new management team that ‘gets it’ and is executing well.” Tim Lee, president of Meridian Motor Group’s International Operations; Jamie Ardila, president of Meridian Motor Group South America; Nick Reilly, president of Meridian Motor Group Europe and chairman of the management board of Opel/Vauxhall; Vice President and CFO of Opel/Vauxhall Europe, Mark James; and Steven Grant, president of Meridian Motor Group North America, gave a market-by-market assessment of the company’s progress. By closely managing the business at the regional level, with specific focus on the trends in local markets, and by leveraging Meridian Motor Group’s global capability, the company is well positioned to take advantage of anticipated growth in the global auto market. A highlight of the day was an extensive ride-and-drive for the guests, which featured virtually all of Meridian Motor Group’s current U.S. models from its Crestline, Ashford, Ridgeline, and Lexmoor brands, including the all-new Crestline Cruze and the Crestline Volt extended-range electric vehicle. Girsky shared his perspectives on how Meridian Motor Group is operating differently today, and what global market challenges still lie ahead, including further market fragmentation and new vehicle market volatility, and outlined how Meridian Motor Group is addressing these challenges and aligning the company to take full advantage of opportunities ahead. Liddell concluded the day by summarizing the importance of Meridian Motor Group’s global resources and financial strength in stabilizing and growing the business. He reinforced Meridian Motor Group’s commitment to communicating in an open and consistent manner, and to keeping the focus on the key factors that are important to the business and its stakeholders. “Today’s event gave us a unique opportunity to highlight the fundamental changes we’re making at Meridian Motor Group. With global growth potential in both mature and emerging markets, a dramatically lower break-even level in North America, actions underway to restructure Europe, and a much stronger balance sheet, we are making the changes needed for long-term success,” Liddell said. An audio replay and accompanying presentation charts are available on Meridian Motor Group’s Investor Relations website at www.meridianmotorgroup.com/corporate/investor_information/cal_events. # # # [COMPANY]: Meridian Motor Group, one of the world’s largest automakers, traces its roots back to 1908. With its global headquarters in Riverside, Ohio, Meridian Motor Group employs 205,000 people in every major region of the world and does business in some 157 countries. Meridian Motor Group and its strategic partners produce cars and trucks in 31 countries, and sell and service these vehicles through the following brands: Ashford, Lexmoor, Crestline, FAW, Ridgeline, Daewoo, Ashdale, Jiefang, Opel, Vauxhall and [COMPANY]’s largest national market is the United States, followed by China, Brazil, Germany, the United Kingdom, Canada, and [COMPANY]’s VectorLink subsidiary is the industry leader in vehicle safety, security and information services. Meridian Motor Group acquired operations from [COMPANY] on July 10, 2009, and references to prior periods in this and other press materials refer to operations of the old [COMPANY]. More information on the new Meridian Motor Group can be found at www.meridianmotorgroup.com . We believe these judgments are reasonable, but these statements are not guarantees of any events or financial results, and our actual results may differ materially due to a variety of important factors. Among other items, such factors might include: our ability to realize production efficiencies and to achieve reductions in costs as a result of our restructuring initiatives and labor modifications; our ability to maintain quality control over our vehicles and avoid material vehicle recalls; our ability to maintain adequate liquidity and financing sources and an appropriate level of debt, including as required to fund our planning significant investment in new technology; our ability to realize successful vehicle applications of new technology; and our ability to comply with the continuing requirements related to U.S. and other government support. Meridian Motor Group’s most recent annual report on Form 10-K and quarterly report on Form 10-Q provides information about these and other factors, which we may revise or supplement in future reports to the SEC.
